author: boma_naps
content: Hi <@194432762315407360> , there's a case in the Hive rpc-compat simulator where checkJSONStructure does a literal case-sensitive key lookup  so when txpool_content returns address keys, the casing has to match the fixture exactly. The current fixture has checksummed address keys (0x0c2c51a0990AeE1d73C1228de158688341557508) but Besu returns lowercase, causing the missing/unexpected key errors. Since the spec schema allows both cases, should the next call be updating checkJSONStructure in Hive to normalize address key comparison, or updating the fixture to lowercase keys? so besu can do the same  more context here https://github.com/ethereum/execution-apis/pull/758#issuecomment-4294733224

author: lightclient
content: I think you can just make it so the simulator doesn't do case sensitive look up, as long as it follows the schema correctly

author: boma_naps
content: Alright thank you, I will put a pr up for this.

author: .fjl
content: I also talked about this on the last call. We will hit more issues like this because the content of the txpool is arbitrary and depends on prior tests. We have to implement a real schema check in the simulator instead of the `checkJSONStructure` thing.
